user_query: Please set up a small, local CI-style pipeline in this workspace that I can run before publishing my weekly column. Use the provided inputs at input/search_topics.csv and input/config/party.yaml. When I run: bash ci.sh run the pipeline should: (1) perform live search engine queries for each topic in input/search_topics.csv, restricting results to the allowed domains listed in input/config/party.yaml (use a general-purpose web search engine; do not require API keys). Prefer items from the last 7 days if the engine supports time filters; otherwise proceed without filtering. (2) Save raw per-topic search results (at minimum title and URL for each hit, plus any available publication date) to JSON files under out/raw_search/<topic>.json. (3) Transform the raw data into a single deduplicated CSV at out/references.csv with columns: topic, title, url, source_domain, published_date (YYYY-MM-DD if available, otherwise empty), found_at (ISO 8601 timestamp of when you found it). Only include rows whose url domain is one of the allowed domains. (4) Compute aggregate stats and write out/stats/summary.json containing: generated_at (ISO 8601), total_results_all, by_domain (list of {domain, count, share where share = count/total_results_all rounded to 3 decimals}), and by_topic (list of {topic, count, top_domain where top_domain is the domain with the most results for that topic, ties broken alphabetically}). Also include: - A test/validation step runnable with: bash ci.sh test that: (a) verifies the CSV and JSON have exactly the fields above, (b) recomputes counts from out/references.csv and asserts they match out/stats/summary.json, and (c) fails if any row’s source_domain is not in the allowed list from input/config/party.yaml. - A clean step: bash ci.sh clean that removes the out/ directory. Notes: - The live Internet search is required for bash ci.sh run; do not hardcode or bundle results. Handle zero-result cases gracefully (still emit the files with headers and totals of 0, and make tests pass). - Use only the provided input files to decide topics and allowed domains; do not add other domains. Deliverables: - ci.sh (POSIX shell is fine), any supporting scripts, and the generated artifacts under out/. I will verify by running: bash ci.sh clean && bash ci.sh run && bash ci.sh test and expect to see out/raw_search/, out/references.csv, and out/stats/summary.json with internally consistent counts. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
